title: Maintain the spend-pool catalogue
description: Create and maintain stable spend-pool identities independently from accounts, payment instruments, cardholders, and spend categories. Implements G12 and UC-LEDGER-018 under C21.
rationale: Company-paid discretionary spending and personal after-tax spending require first-class pool identities rather than category or account conventions.
story_want: maintain the spend pools used to separate funding contexts
story_so_that: budget and insight consumers can report exact pool totals without inferring them from other dimensions
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid unique pool name such as company-paid discretionary or personal after-tax, when the owner creates a spend pool, then one active pool with a stable identifier is returned.
Given an existing spend pool, when the owner renames it, then its stable identifier and historical transaction assignments remain unchanged.
Given a referenced spend pool, when the owner archives it, then history remains queryable and new assignments are rejected until the same identity is reactivated.

Given account, payment instrument, cardholder, category, and pool records, when any is created or changed, then no value in one dimension silently creates, selects, renames, or archives a value in another.
Given active and archived pools, when they are listed with an explicit lifecycle filter, then each matching pool appears once with stable identity and assignment counts.
security_criteria: Pool labels and metadata must remain provider-neutral and must not encode credentials or unmasked account or instrument identifiers.
